title: KAP INFRA-4B Report Format
content: The final report for KAP INFRA-4B should include the following lines: 12. raw tokens captured: yes/no/local_restricted_only 13. raw token files committed to GitHub: yes/no 14. redacted token registry committed: yes/no

title: Universal and Permanent Access Resolution
content: Prioritize and implement a permanent, robust, and universal solution for access management across all critical platforms. This includes ensuring seamless and reliable authentication for Git, Notion API, Manus API, or Mem0, and any other...

title: Feedback for Architect Chat GPT
content: Always provide detailed feedback in a Markdown file format, available for download, to 'architect Chat GPT'.

title: yOS Core Orchestration - Context Continuity
content: Context continuity is a core and essential module within yOS Core Orchestration, representing a significant value-added feature. It is crucial for seamless inter-LLM context transfer.

title: yOS LLM & Tool Routing Matrix Requirements
content: The yOS LLM & Tool Routing Matrix must be agnostic across all program types and distinguish between book prose, architecture, image, video, charts, research, code, slides, and translation. It should include context window, output window, cost, latency, quality risk, and fallback rules (auto-fallback vs. approval-required fallback). Additionally, it needs to include QC requirements per task type and an update policy based on yOS empirical learning. Legacy skipped files must be reported as QC debt, not silently ignored. A transitional compatibility bridge should be added for the yOS LLM & Tool Routing Matrix, and there should not be two silent copies. Do not start F02, generate book prose, modify manuscript prose, implement the full yOS Orchestration Core runtime, or delete/move files in a way that could break existing scripts. The yOS Orchestration Core now canonically owns the yOS LLM & Tool Routing Matrix.
